questionHtml:
Find a minimum spanning tree for the graph using Kruskal’s algorithm.

State clearly the order in which your edges are added, and draw the tree obtained.
markschemeHtml:
edge CD selected first          M1 
        DN,
        CL,
        LS          A1

 

Note: Award marks if the answers are written as sums in the correct order. M1 if 30 is seen first, A1 for 30+39+41+58.

 

         A1

 

Note: The final A1 can be awarded independently. Award M0A0A1 for a correct MST graph with no other working. Award M1A0A1 if Prim’s algorithm is seen to be used correctly with CD first.

 

[3 marks]

meta.title: Find a minimum spanning tree for the graph using Kruskal’s algorithm. State clearly the order in which your edges are added, and draw the tree obtained.
breadcrumbLabels: AHL 3.16—Tree and cycle algorithms, Chinese postman, travelling salesman
